title: CLAUDE.md
text: file provides guidance Claude Code (claude.ai/code) working code repository.

title: CLAUDE.md
text: mdgm (Mixture Directed Graphical Models) R package C++20 core library. implements graph data structures sampling algorithms probabilistic graphical models. C++ layer handles computation; R layer provides user-facing APIs via cpp11 bindings R6 classes. paper available https://arxiv.org/html/2406.15700v4.

title: CLAUDE.md
code: R CMD build . R CMD INSTALL .

title: CLAUDE.md
text: Run single test suite:
code: cmake -B build -DBUILD_TESTS=ON cmake --build build ctest --test-dir build cmake --build build && ./build/tests/cpp/<test_binary>

title: CLAUDE.md
text: modifying [[cpp11::register]] annotated functions src/R_*.cpp: updates src/cpp11.cpp R/cpp11.R.
code: cpp11::cpp_register()

title: CLAUDE.md
text: Graph Storage (graph_storage.hpp/cpp) — Two sparse formats: GraphCOO (coordinate/edge list) GraphCSR (compressed sparse row). COO used construction, CSR efficient queries. Conversion transposition supported. NaturalUndirectedGraph (natural_undirected_graph.hpp/cpp) — Undirected graph built GraphCSR. Key algorithms: SampleSpanningTree() (Wilson’s, Aldous-Broder, FastForward methods) SampleAcyclicOrientation(). GenerateRegularGraph() creates 2D grid graphs. DirectedAcyclicGraph (directed_acyclic_graph.hpp/cpp) — DAG forward (CSR) reverse (transposed) representations parent/child queries. RNG (rng.hpp) — Wrapper around std::mt19937_64 providing uniform, normal, discrete, permutation sampling.

title: CLAUDE.md
text: cpp11 bindings (src/R_natural_undirected_graph.cpp, src/R_utils.cpp) — C++ objects wrapped R external pointers. Functions annotated [[cpp11::register]] auto-registered. R6 class (R/natural_undirected_graph.R) — NaturalUndirectedGraph R6 class wrapping C++ object. User-facing constructors: nug_from_edge_list(), nug_from_adj_list(), nug_from_adj_mat(). Indexing convention: R uses 1-indexed vectors; conversion 0-indexed C++ happens R binding layer (R_natural_undirected_graph.cpp).

title: CLAUDE.md
text: C++ unit tests (GoogleTest). Three test files mirror core classes: graph storage, undirected graphs, DAGs.

title: CLAUDE.md
text: Directed edges follow standard ordered pair: (parent, child) (paper linked currently reverse (child, parent)) C++20 standard required (CXX_STD = CXX20 src/Makevars) Public C++ headers live inst/include/mdgm/ (R package convention header-/shared headers) Enum-based algorithm dispatch (e.g., SpanningTreeMethod::kWilson) Value semantics throughout C++ classes

title: Emission Models
text: hierarchical MDGM, spatial field zz latent observations yy generated emission distribution. mdgm package supports three emission families: emission families enforce identifiability constraint location parameters: p0<p1<⋯p_0 < p_1 < \cdots (Bernoulli), μ0<μ1<⋯\mu_0 < \mu_1 < \cdots (Gaussian), λ0<λ1<⋯\lambda_0 < \lambda_1 < \cdots (Poisson). achieved via truncated conjugate posterior sampling.

title: Emission Models
text: examples use simple region-growing algorithm generate irregular spatial clusters grid graph. Starting random seed vertices, regions expand outward one cell time, producing blocky asymmetric patterns:
code: grow_regions <- function(nug, n_colors, seed = NULL) {   if (!is.null(seed)) set.seed(seed)   n <- nug$nvertices()   z <- rep(NA_integer_, n)    # Plant one seed per color   seeds <- sample(n, n_colors)   for (k in seq_along(seeds)) z[seeds[k]] <- k - 1L    # Grow: repeatedly claim a random unclaimed neighbor   repeat {     boundary <- which(!is.na(z))     candidates <- integer(0)     for (v in boundary) {       nbrs <- nug$neighbors(v)       candidates <- c(candidates, nbrs[is.na(z[nbrs])])     }     candidates <- unique(candidates)     if (length(candidates) == 0) break      pick <- sample(candidates, 1)     claimed <- nug$neighbors(pick)     claimed <- claimed[!is.na(z[claimed])]     z[pick] <- z[sample(claimed, 1)]   }   z } nug <- nug_from_grid(8, 8, seed = 42L) n <- nug$nvertices()  z_true <- grow_regions(nug, n_colors = 2, seed = 7)  grid_df <- data.frame(   x = rep(1:8, 8), y = rep(8:1, each = 8),   z = factor(z_true) ) ggplot(grid_df, aes(x, y, fill = z)) +   geom_tile(color = "white", linewidth = 0.3) +   scale_fill_manual(values = c("0" = "#440154", "1" = "#fde725")) +   coord_equal() + theme_minimal() +   labs(title = "Simulated spatial field (region growing)", fill = "z")

title: Emission Models
text: Bernoulli emission models binary observations. vertex ii can multiple replicate observations yi1,…,yimiy_{i1}, \ldots, y_{im_i}, drawn independently Bernoulli(pzi)\text{Bernoulli}(p_{z_i}). Multiple replicates per vertex needed identifiability Bernoulli case.

what: Example: binary indicators on a grid
title: Emission Models
text: Fit model:
code: set.seed(1) p_true <- c(0.3, 0.7)  # 5 replicate observations per vertex y_bern <- lapply(seq_len(n), function(i) {   rbinom(5, 1, p_true[z_true[i] + 1]) }) model_b <- mdgm_model(nug, dag_type = "spanning_tree",                        n_colors = 2L, emission = "bernoulli")  result_b <- mcmc(model_b, y = y_bern,                  z_init = sample(0:1, n, replace = TRUE),                  psi_init = 0.5,                  theta_init = c(0.3, 0.7),                  n_iter = 2000L,                  psi_tune = 1.0,                  seed = 42L,                  nug = nug)  result_b$summary(burnin = 500L) #> MDGM MCMC Results #>   Vertices: 64, Colors: 2 #>   Iterations: 2000 (burnin: 500) #>   Psi acceptance rate: 0.465 #>   Psi posterior mean: 2.2008 (sd: 1.3132) #>   Emission type: bernoulli #>   p_1 posterior mean: 0.4141 (sd: 0.1506) #>   p_2 posterior mean: 0.7350 (sd: 0.0432) #>   Psi R-hat: 1.0953, ESS: 11

title: Emission Models
code: ep_b <- result_b$emission_params() n_iter <- ncol(ep_b$p)  p_df <- data.frame(   iteration = rep(seq_len(n_iter), 2),   value = c(ep_b$p[1, ], ep_b$p[2, ]),   parameter = rep(c("p_1", "p_2"), each = n_iter) )  ggplot(p_df, aes(iteration, value, color = parameter)) +   geom_line(alpha = 0.5) +   geom_hline(yintercept = p_true, linetype = "dashed", alpha = 0.5) +   theme_minimal() +   labs(title = "Bernoulli emission: p trace",        x = "Iteration", y = "Value", color = NULL)

title: Emission Models
text: Gaussian emission models continuous (integer-valued) observations. observation drawn 𝒩(μzi,σzi2)\mathcal{N}(\mu_{z_i}, \sigma_{z_i}^2). single observation per vertex sufficient identifiability. priors μk\mu_k σk2\sigma_k^2 independent: μk∼𝒩(μ0,σ02),σk2∼InverseGamma(α0,β0)\mu_k \sim \mathcal{N}(\mu_0, \sigma^2_0), \qquad \sigma_k^2 \sim \text{InverseGamma}(\alpha_0, \beta_0) σ02\sigma^2_0 prior variance mean.

title: Emission Models
text: use overlapping emission distributions — two groups differ mean share similar spread, making classification rely emission signal spatial structure:  Fit Gaussian emission:
code: set.seed(2) mu_true <- c(5, 12) sigma2_true <- c(9, 9)  # Single observation per vertex y_gauss <- lapply(seq_len(n), function(i) {   as.integer(round(rnorm(1, mu_true[z_true[i] + 1],                          sqrt(sigma2_true[z_true[i] + 1])))) })  # Visualize the observations obs_df <- data.frame(   x = rep(1:8, 8), y = rep(8:1, each = 8),   value = vapply(y_gauss, `[`, integer(1), 1) ) ggplot(obs_df, aes(x, y, fill = value)) +   geom_tile(color = "white", linewidth = 0.3) +   scale_fill_gradient2(low = "#440154", mid = "#21918c", high = "#fde725",                        midpoint = mean(obs_df$value)) +   coord_equal() + theme_minimal() +   labs(title = "Observed temperatures (single obs per vertex)", fill = "y") model_g <- mdgm_model(nug, dag_type = "spanning_tree",                        n_colors = 2L, emission = "gaussian")  # theta_init: c(mu_1, mu_2, sigma2_1, sigma2_2) result_g <- mcmc(model_g, y = y_gauss,                  z_init = sample(0:1, n, replace = TRUE),                  psi_init = 0.5,                  theta_init = c(4, 14, 9, 9),                  n_iter = 2000L,                  psi_tune = 1.0,                  seed = 42L,                  nug = nug)  result_g$summary(burnin = 500L) #> MDGM MCMC Results #>   Vertices: 64, Colors: 2 #>   Iterations: 2000 (burnin: 500) #>   Psi acceptance rate: 0.513 #>   Psi posterior mean: 2.9534 (sd: 1.3112) #>   Emission type: gaussian #>   mu_1 posterior mean: 2.6923 (sd: 2.0341) #>   sigma2_1 posterior mean: 12.6376 (sd: 0.6073) #>   NA posterior mean: 10.7035 (sd: 11.4432) #>   NA posterior mean: 12.2790 (sd: 3.0145) #>   Psi R-hat: 1.0001, ESS: 51

title: Emission Models
code: ep_g <- result_g$emission_params() n_iter_g <- ncol(ep_g$mu)  mu_df <- data.frame(   iteration = rep(seq_len(n_iter_g), 2),   value = c(ep_g$mu[1, ], ep_g$mu[2, ]),   parameter = rep(c("mu_1", "mu_2"), each = n_iter_g) )  ggplot(mu_df, aes(iteration, value, color = parameter)) +   geom_line(alpha = 0.5) +   geom_hline(yintercept = mu_true, linetype = "dashed", alpha = 0.5) +   theme_minimal() +   labs(title = "Gaussian emission: mu trace",        x = "Iteration", y = "Value", color = NULL) sigma2_df <- data.frame(   iteration = rep(seq_len(n_iter_g), 2),   value = c(ep_g$sigma2[1, ], ep_g$sigma2[2, ]),   parameter = rep(c("sigma2_1", "sigma2_2"), each = n_iter_g) )  ggplot(sigma2_df, aes(iteration, value, color = parameter)) +   geom_line(alpha = 0.5) +   geom_hline(yintercept = sigma2_true, linetype = "dashed", alpha = 0.5) +   theme_minimal() +   labs(title = "Gaussian emission: sigma2 trace",        x = "Iteration", y = "Value", color = NULL)

title: Emission Models
text: Poisson emission models count data. observation drawn Poisson(λzi)\text{Poisson}(\lambda_{z_i}). Gaussian case, single observation per vertex sufficient identifiability. prior Gamma distribution: λk∼Gamma(α0,β0)\lambda_k \sim \text{Gamma}(\alpha_0, \beta_0) rate parameterization (mean =α0/β0= \alpha_0 / \beta_0).

title: Emission Models
text: use overlapping rates spatial structure contributes meaningfully classification:  Fit Poisson emission:
code: set.seed(3) lambda_true <- c(4, 10)  # Single count per vertex y_pois <- lapply(seq_len(n), function(i) {   as.integer(rpois(1, lambda_true[z_true[i] + 1])) })  obs_df_p <- data.frame(   x = rep(1:8, 8), y = rep(8:1, each = 8),   value = vapply(y_pois, `[`, integer(1), 1) ) ggplot(obs_df_p, aes(x, y, fill = value)) +   geom_tile(color = "white", linewidth = 0.3) +   scale_fill_gradient(low = "#440154", high = "#fde725") +   coord_equal() + theme_minimal() +   labs(title = "Observed counts (single obs per vertex)", fill = "y") model_p <- mdgm_model(nug, dag_type = "spanning_tree",                        n_colors = 2L, emission = "poisson")  result_p <- mcmc(model_p, y = y_pois,                  z_init = sample(0:1, n, replace = TRUE),                  psi_init = 0.5,                  theta_init = c(3, 8),                  n_iter = 2000L,                  psi_tune = 1.0,                  seed = 42L,                  nug = nug)  result_p$summary(burnin = 500L) #> MDGM MCMC Results #>   Vertices: 64, Colors: 2 #>   Iterations: 2000 (burnin: 500) #>   Psi acceptance rate: 0.437 #>   Psi posterior mean: 1.8821 (sd: 1.2732) #>   Emission type: poisson #>   lambda_1 posterior mean: 5.4345 (sd: 1.7691) #>   lambda_2 posterior mean: 9.9390 (sd: 0.8308) #>   Psi R-hat: 1.0040, ESS: 29

title: Emission Models
code: ep_p <- result_p$emission_params() n_iter_p <- ncol(ep_p$lambda)  lambda_df <- data.frame(   iteration = rep(seq_len(n_iter_p), 2),   value = c(ep_p$lambda[1, ], ep_p$lambda[2, ]),   parameter = rep(c("lambda_1", "lambda_2"), each = n_iter_p) )  ggplot(lambda_df, aes(iteration, value, color = parameter)) +   geom_line(alpha = 0.5) +   geom_hline(yintercept = lambda_true, linetype = "dashed", alpha = 0.5) +   theme_minimal() +   labs(title = "Poisson emission: lambda trace",        x = "Iteration", y = "Value", color = NULL)

title: Emission Models
text: three emission types, posterior mode latent field recover true spatial pattern:
code: burnin <- 500L  recover_field <- function(result, burnin) {   z_post <- result$z()   z_burn <- z_post[, (burnin + 1):ncol(z_post)]   apply(z_burn, 1, function(row) {     tbl <- tabulate(row + 1L, nbins = 2)     which.max(tbl) - 1L   }) }  z_mode_b <- recover_field(result_b, burnin) z_mode_g <- recover_field(result_g, burnin) z_mode_p <- recover_field(result_p, burnin)  field_df <- data.frame(   x = rep(rep(1:8, 8), 4),   y = rep(rep(8:1, each = 8), 4),   value = c(z_true, z_mode_b, z_mode_g, z_mode_p),   panel = rep(c("True field", "Bernoulli", "Gaussian", "Poisson"),               each = n) ) field_df$panel <- factor(field_df$panel,                          levels = c("True field", "Bernoulli",                                     "Gaussian", "Poisson"))  ggplot(field_df, aes(x, y, fill = factor(value))) +   geom_tile(color = "white", linewidth = 0.2) +   scale_fill_manual(values = c("0" = "#440154", "1" = "#fde725")) +   coord_equal() +   facet_wrap(~panel, nrow = 1) +   theme_minimal() +   labs(fill = "z")

title: Emission Models
text: Bernoulli c(, b): Use c(1, 1) (uniform) default. Increase b shrink pkp_k toward 0.5 expect weak signal. Gaussian c(mu_0, sigma2_0, alpha_0, beta_0): Set mu_0 near data mean, sigma2_0 large (e.g., 10000) vague location prior, alpha_0 = 2, beta_0 near expected variance weakly informative scale prior. Poisson c(alpha_0, beta_0): prior mean alpha_0 / beta_0. Use c(1, 0.1) diffuse prior mean 10, match expected count range.

title: Working with Undirected Graphs
text: NaturalUndirectedGraph foundational graph object mdgm. stores undirected graph compressed sparse row (CSR) format provides methods querying structure sampling spanning trees. Three constructor functions create graphs different representations.

title: Working with Undirected Graphs
text: Edge lists matrices (data frames) two three columns. first two columns 1-indexed vertex pairs. undirected edge must appear twice (per direction). optional third column gives edge weights.
code: # Triangle: 1 -- 2 -- 3 -- 1 edges <- rbind(   c(1, 2), c(2, 1),   c(2, 3), c(3, 2),   c(1, 3), c(3, 1) ) g <- nug_from_edge_list(3, edges) g$nvertices() #> [1] 3 g$nedges() #> [1] 3

title: Working with Undirected Graphs
text: adjacency list list -th element integer vector vertex ’s neighbors.
code: # 4-vertex star: vertex 1 connected to 2, 3, 4 adj <- list(   c(2L, 3L, 4L),   1L,   1L,   1L ) g <- nug_from_adj_list(adj) g$nvertices() #> [1] 4 g$nedges() #> [1] 3

title: Working with Undirected Graphs
text: symmetric matrix nonzero entries indicate edges. entry values used edge weights.
code: # 3x3 grid (rook adjacency) n <- 9 A <- matrix(0, n, n) for (i in 1:n) {   row_i <- (i - 1) %/% 3 + 1   col_i <- (i - 1) %% 3 + 1   for (j in 1:n) {     row_j <- (j - 1) %/% 3 + 1     col_j <- (j - 1) %% 3 + 1     if (abs(row_i - row_j) + abs(col_i - col_j) == 1) {       A[i, j] <- 1     }   } } g <- nug_from_adj_mat(A, seed = 123L) g$nvertices() #> [1] 9 g$nedges() #> [1] 12

title: Working with Undirected Graphs
code: g$nvertices() #> [1] 9 g$nedges() #> [1] 12  # Neighbors of vertex 5 (center of 3x3 grid) — should be 2, 4, 6, 8 sort(g$neighbors(5)) #> [1] 2 4 6 8

title: Working with Undirected Graphs
text: Spanning trees sampled uniformly (edge weights) using Wilson’s algorithm, Aldous-Broder, fast-forward hybrid method. Available methods: "wilson" — Wilson’s algorithm using loop-erased random walks. Efficient graphs. "aldous_broder" — Aldous-Broder algorithm using simple random walk. Simpler slower large graphs. "fast_forward" — Hybrid method switches random walk loop-erased walk.
code: # Sample with Wilson's algorithm (default) tree <- g$sample_spanning_tree("wilson")

title: Working with Undirected Graphs
text: igraph package installed, can convert plot graph.
code: library(igraph) #>  #> Attaching package: 'igraph' #> The following objects are masked from 'package:stats': #>  #>     decompose, spectrum #> The following object is masked from 'package:base': #>  #>     union  # Convert adjacency matrix to igraph ig <- graph_from_adjacency_matrix(A, mode = "undirected")  # Layout as grid coords <- cbind((1:n - 1) %% 3, (1:n - 1) %/% 3) plot(ig, layout = coords, vertex.size = 20, vertex.label = 1:n,      main = "3x3 Grid Graph")

title: MDGM Model Specification
text: Classical spatial models discrete data typically use Markov random field (MRF), specifies joint distribution undirected graph. However, MRF likelihood involves intractable normalizing constant (partition function), making exact Bayesian inference computationally burdensome. Mixture Directed Graphical Models (MDGM) alternative takes undirected graph input defines mixture compatible directed acyclic graphs (DAGs). DAG admits tractable factorization, MDGM avoids partition function entirely. joint distribution marginalizes DAG space: p(𝐳∣𝛏)=∑Dp(D)p(𝐳∣D,𝛏)p(\mathbf{z} \mid \boldsymbol{\xi}) = \sum_D p(D)\, p(\mathbf{z} \mid D, \boldsymbol{\xi}). full details, see Carter Calder (2024).

title: MDGM Model Specification
text: Let G=(V,E)G = (V, E) undirected graph (“natural undirected graph” NUG) encoding potential neighbor relationships. MDGM places prior DAGs DD compatible GG: every directed edge (u,v)(u, v) DD corresponds undirected edge {u,v}\{u, v\} GG. Three DAG constructions supported:

title: MDGM Model Specification
text: spanning tree TT GG connected, acyclic subgraph containing vertices. Edges directed child parent. posterior sampling uses Wilson’s algorithm data-dependent edge weights: w(u,v)=exp(ψ⋅𝟏[zu=zv])w(u, v) = \exp\bigl(\psi \cdot \mathbf{1}[z_u = z_v]\bigr) ψ>0\psi > 0 spatial dependence parameter zz color assignment. provides direct (non-MH) posterior sample spanning tree.

title: MDGM Model Specification
text: acyclic orientation assigns direction every edge GG directed cycle exists. Equivalently, defined vertex permutation σ\sigma: edge {u,v}\{u, v\} directed (u,v)(u, v) σ(u)>σ(v)\sigma(u) > \sigma(v). MCMC proposes new permutations accepts via Metropolis-Hastings step based exact DAG log-likelihood ratio.

title: MDGM Model Specification
text: rooted DAG constructed GG choosing root vertex orienting edges via breadth-first depth-first traversal. MCMC updates root via random walk proposals GG.

title: MDGM Model Specification
text: Given DAG DD, vertex ii set parents paD()\text{pa}_D(). conditional distribution color ziz_i given parents : $$p(z_i = k \mid z_{\text{pa}()}, \psi) \propto   \exp\Bigl(\psi \sum_{j \\text{pa}()} \mathbf{1}[z_j = k] + \alpha_k\Bigr)$$ αk\alpha_k marginal log-probabilities (currently fixed 0 uniform marginal).

title: MDGM Model Specification
text: standalone model, spatial field zz observed directly. MCMC updates DAG structure DD dependence parameter ψ\psi. useful data categorical labels spatial domain.

title: MDGM Model Specification
text: hierarchical model, zz latent field observations yiy_i generated emission distribution: yij∣zi,θ∼f(yij∣θzi)y_{ij} \mid z_i, \theta \sim f(y_{ij} \mid \theta_{z_i}) Currently supported emission families: Bernoulli: yij∣zi=k∼Bernoulli(pk)y_{ij} \mid z_i = k \sim \text{Bernoulli}(p_k), identifiability constraint p0<p1<⋯p_0 < p_1 < \cdots enforced via truncated Beta posterior sampling. Gaussian: yij∣zi=k∼𝒩(μk,σk2)y_{ij} \mid z_i = k \sim \mathcal{N}(\mu_k, \sigma_k^2), identifiability constraint μ0<μ1<⋯\mu_0 < \mu_1 < \cdots. Independent Normal Inverse-Gamma conjugate updates μk\mu_k σk2\sigma_k^2. Poisson: yij∣zi=k∼Poisson(λk)y_{ij} \mid z_i = k \sim \text{Poisson}(\lambda_k), identifiability constraint λ0<λ1<⋯\lambda_0 < \lambda_1 < \cdots. Conjugate truncated Gamma updates. MCMC additionally updates zz (Gibbs scan vertices) emission parameters (conjugate updates). See Emission Models vignette worked examples.

title: MDGM Model Specification
text: spatial dependence parameter ψ>0\psi > 0 half-Cauchy prior: p(ψ)=2π(1+ψ2),ψ>0p(\psi) = \frac{2}{\pi(1 + \psi^2)}, \quad \psi > 0 Updates use Metropolis-Hastings random walk normal proposal.

title: MDGM Model Specification
text: Bernoulli: pkp_k Beta(,b)\text{Beta}(, b) prior. emission_prior_params = c(, b). Gaussian: μk∼𝒩(μ0,σ02)\mu_k \sim \mathcal{N}(\mu_0, \sigma^2_0) σk2∼InverseGamma(α0,β0)\sigma_k^2 \sim \text{InverseGamma}(\alpha_0, \beta_0), independently. emission_prior_params = c(mu_0, sigma2_0, alpha_0, beta_0). Poisson: λk\lambda_k Gamma(α0,β0)\text{Gamma}(\alpha_0, \beta_0) prior (rate parameterization). emission_prior_params = c(alpha_0, beta_0). conjugate posteriors use truncated sampling enforce parameter ordering identifiability.

title: MDGM Model Specification
text: iteration MCMC sampler performs: Update DAG DD — Sample new spanning tree (direct posterior sample via Wilson’s) propose new acyclic orientation/root (MH step). Update zz (hierarchical ) — Gibbs scan vertices random order. full conditional combines spatial prior emission likelihood. Update ψ\psi — Metropolis-Hastings normal random walk proposal. Update θ\theta (hierarchical ) — Conjugate posterior sampling identifiability constraints.

title: Getting Started with mdgm
text: mdgm package implements Bayesian inference discrete spatial random fields using Mixtures Directed Graphical Models. Like Markov random field (MRF), MDGM takes undirected graph input represent spatial structure. However, instead specifying single joint distribution intractable normalizing constant, MDGM defines mixture directed acyclic graphs (DAGs) compatible undirected graph. DAG admits efficient factorization, avoiding computational burden MRF partition function providing valid probabilistic inference. approach described : Carter, J. B. Calder, C. . (2024). Mixture Directed Graphical Models Discrete Spatial Random Fields. arXiv:2406.15700

title: Getting Started with mdgm
text: Create 4x4 grid graph rook adjacency using nug_from_grid(): Graphs can also constructed adjacency matrix, edge list, adjacency list — see Working Undirected Graphs vignette.
code: nug <- nug_from_grid(4, 4, seed = 42L) nug$nvertices() #> [1] 16 nug$nedges() #> [1] 24

title: Getting Started with mdgm
text: igraph package (suggested, required) provides quick way visualize neighborhood structure:
code: library(igraph) #>  #> Attaching package: 'igraph' #> The following objects are masked from 'package:stats': #>  #>     decompose, spectrum #> The following object is masked from 'package:base': #>  #>     union  # Build igraph object from the NUG edge structure n <- nug$nvertices() el <- do.call(rbind, lapply(1:n, function(v) {   nbrs <- nug$neighbors(v)   nbrs <- nbrs[nbrs > v]   if (length(nbrs) == 0) return(NULL)   cbind(v, nbrs) })) g <- graph_from_edgelist(el, directed = FALSE)  # Grid layout matching the spatial positions coords <- cbind((seq_len(n) - 1) %% 4 + 1, 4 - (seq_len(n) - 1) %/% 4)  plot(g, layout = coords, vertex.size = 20, vertex.label = 1:n,      vertex.label.color = "white", vertex.color = "steelblue",      edge.color = "grey40", main = "4x4 Grid Graph")

title: Getting Started with mdgm
text: standalone model, spatial field zz observed directly — emission distribution needed. fit spanning-tree MDGM deterministic checkerboard pattern 4x4 grid: can color graph vertices field values:  visualize raster:
code: z <- c(0L, 0L, 0L, 1L,        0L, 0L, 1L, 1L,        1L, 1L, 1L, 0L,        1L, 1L, 0L, 0L)  model <- mdgm_model(nug, dag_type = "spanning_tree")  result <- mcmc(model, z_init = z, psi_init = 0.5,                n_iter = 2000L, psi_tune = 1.0, seed = 42L) result$summary() #> MDGM MCMC Results #>   Vertices: 16, Colors: 2 #>   Iterations: 2000 (burnin: 0) #>   Psi acceptance rate: 0.478 #>   Psi posterior mean: 0.8446 (sd: 0.5612) #>   Psi R-hat: 1.0013, ESS: 272 plot(g, layout = coords, vertex.size = 20, vertex.label = 1:n,      vertex.color = c("#440154", "#fde725")[z + 1],      vertex.label.color = "white", edge.color = "grey40",      main = "Spatial Field on Grid") grid_df <- data.frame(   x = rep(1:4, times = 4),   y = rep(4:1, each = 4),   z = factor(z) )  ggplot(grid_df, aes(x, y, fill = z)) +   geom_raster() +   scale_fill_manual(values = c("0" = "#440154", "1" = "#fde725")) +   coord_equal() +   theme_minimal() +   labs(title = "4x4 Spatial Field", fill = "z")

title: Getting Started with mdgm
text: Trace plot spatial dependence parameter ψ\psi:  Acceptance rates:
code: psi_df <- data.frame(iteration = seq_along(result$psi()), psi = result$psi())  ggplot(psi_df, aes(iteration, psi)) +   geom_line(linewidth = 0.3) +   theme_minimal() +   labs(x = "Iteration", y = expression(psi), title = "Psi trace plot") result$acceptance_rates() #>       psi     graph  #> 0.4777389 0.0000000

title: Getting Started with mdgm
text: edge_inclusion_probs() method counts often undirected edge appears posterior spanning-tree samples. can use proportions scale edge widths:  Edges -colored vertices appear frequently posterior spanning trees, reflecting spatial dependence captured ψ\psi.
code: eip <- result$edge_inclusion_probs(nug, burnin = 200L) head(eip[order(-eip$prob), ]) #>    vertex1 vertex2      prob #> 7        4       8 0.8116667 #> 21      12      16 0.8111111 #> 24      15      16 0.7794444 #> 3        2       3 0.7700000 #> 1        1       2 0.7238889 #> 2        1       5 0.7177778  # Match edge inclusion probs to igraph edge ordering edge_probs <- numeric(ecount(g)) for (i in seq_len(nrow(eip))) {   eid <- get.edge.ids(g, c(eip$vertex1[i], eip$vertex2[i]))   edge_probs[eid] <- eip$prob[i] } #> Warning: `get.edge.ids()` was deprecated in igraph 2.1.0. #> ℹ Please use `get_edge_ids()` instead. #> This warning is displayed once per session. #> Call `lifecycle::last_lifecycle_warnings()` to see where this warning was #> generated.  plot(g, layout = coords, vertex.size = 20, vertex.label = 1:n,      vertex.color = c("#440154", "#fde725")[z + 1],      vertex.label.color = "white",      edge.width = edge_probs * 10,      edge.color = "grey30",      main = "Edge inclusion probabilities")

title: Getting Started with mdgm
text: hierarchical model, zz latent field observations generated emission distribution. use Bernoulli emission:
code: model_h <- mdgm_model(nug, dag_type = "spanning_tree", n_colors = 2L,                        emission = "bernoulli")  # Simulate 5 Bernoulli observations per vertex (multiple needed for identifiability) set.seed(1) p_true <- c(0.2, 0.8) y <- lapply(seq_len(n), function(i) rbinom(5, 1, p_true[z[i] + 1]))  result_h <- mcmc(model_h, y = y, z_init = sample(0:1, n, replace = TRUE),                  psi_init = 0.5, theta_init = c(0.3, 0.7),                  n_iter = 500L, seed = 42L) result_h$summary() #> MDGM MCMC Results #>   Vertices: 16, Colors: 2 #>   Iterations: 500 (burnin: 0) #>   Psi acceptance rate: 0.896 #>   Psi posterior mean: 0.6269 (sd: 0.5127) #>   Emission type: bernoulli #>   p_1 posterior mean: 0.2284 (sd: 0.0792) #>   p_2 posterior mean: 0.8089 (sd: 0.0675) #>   Psi R-hat: 1.0124, ESS: 6

title: Getting Started with mdgm
text: Working Undirected Graphs — Graph constructors, querying structure, sampling spanning trees. MDGM Model Specification — DAG types, spatial field model, emission distributions, MCMC details. Emission Models — Worked examples Bernoulli, Gaussian, Poisson emission distributions.

title: Authors and Citation
text: Brandon Carter. Author, maintainer.

title: Authors and Citation
text: Carter B (2026). mdgm: Mixture Directed Graphical Models. R package version 0.1.0, https://jbcart.github.io/mdgm.
code: @Manual{,   title = {mdgm: Mixture of Directed Graphical Models},   author = {Brandon Carter},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.1.0},   url = {https://jbcart.github.io/mdgm}, }

title: Mixture of Directed Graphical Models
text: Full documentation: https://jbcart.github.io/mdgm/ mdgm (Mixture Directed Graphical Models) provides Bayesian inference discrete spatial random fields. Instead working Markov random field intractable normalizing constant, MDGM defines mixture directed acyclic graphs (DAGs) compatible undirected neighborhood graph. DAG admits tractable likelihood, avoiding partition function entirely. See Carter Calder (2024) full methodological details.

title: Mixture of Directed Graphical Models
text: can install development version GitHub: C++20 compiler required.
code: # install.packages("pak") pak::pak("jbcart/mdgm")

title: Mixture of Directed Graphical Models
text: See vignette("mdgm") full walkthrough including visualization posterior diagnostics.
code: library(mdgm)  # Build a 4x4 grid graph with rook adjacency nug <- nug_from_grid(4, 4, seed = 42L) nug$nvertices() #> [1] 16 nug$nedges() #> [1] 24  # Fit a standalone spanning-tree MDGM z <- c(0L, 0L, 0L, 1L,        0L, 0L, 1L, 1L,        1L, 1L, 1L, 0L,        1L, 1L, 0L, 0L) model <- mdgm_model(nug, dag_type = "spanning_tree") result <- mcmc(model, z_init = z, psi_init = 0.5,                n_iter = 2000L, psi_tune = 1.0, seed = 42L) result$summary() #> MDGM MCMC Results #>   Vertices: 16, Colors: 2 #>   Iterations: 2000 (burnin: 0) #>   Psi acceptance rate: 0.462 #>   Psi posterior mean: 0.8447 (sd: 0.5658) #>   Psi R-hat: 1.0035, ESS: 308

title: Mixture of Directed Graphical Models

code: cmake -B build -DBUILD_TESTS=ON cmake --build build ctest --test-dir build

what: Run MCMC inference for an MDGM model — mcmc
title: Run MCMC inference for an MDGM model — mcmc
text: Performs Markov chain Monte Carlo sampling estimate posterior distribution spatial field, DAG structure, dependence parameter, (hierarchical models) emission parameters.

title: Run MCMC inference for an MDGM model — mcmc
code: mcmc(   model,   y = NULL,   z_init,   psi_init,   theta_init = numeric(0),   n_iter = 1000L,   psi_tune = 0.1,   emission_prior_params = NULL,   seed = NULL,   nug = NULL )

title: Run MCMC inference for an MDGM model — mcmc
text: model MdgmModel object created mdgm_model(). y Observation data. hierarchical models, list integer vectors y[[]] contains observations vertex . Vertices observations integer(0). standalone models, pass NULL (default). z_init Initial color assignment, integer vector length n values 0, ..., n_colors - 1. psi_init Initial value dependence parameter (positive). theta_init Initial emission parameters. Bernoulli, numeric vector length n_colors (e.g., c(0.2, 0.8)). Gaussian, vector length 2 * n_colors means variances (e.g., c(mu_1, mu_2, sigma2_1, sigma2_2)). Poisson, vector length n_colors rate parameters. Ignored standalone models. n_iter Number MCMC iterations (default 1000). psi_tune Standard deviation normal MH proposal psi (default 0.1). emission_prior_params Prior hyperparameters emission parameters. Bernoulli: c(, b) Beta(, b) prior. Gaussian: c(mu_0, sigma2_0, alpha_0, beta_0) mu_k ~ N(mu_0, sigma2_0) sigma_k^2 ~ InvGamma(alpha_0, beta_0) independently. Poisson: c(alpha_0, beta_0) Gamma(alpha_0, beta_0) prior. Default: c(1, 1) Bernoulli/Poisson, c(0, 10000, 0.01, 0.01) Gaussian (non-informative). seed Optional integer seed reproducibility. nug Optional NaturalUndirectedGraph object. provided, stored result use edge_inclusion_probs() plot().

title: Run MCMC inference for an MDGM model — mcmc
text: MdgmResult object containing posterior samples.

title: Run MCMC inference for an MDGM model — mcmc
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Create a small grid graph A <- matrix(0, 4, 4) A[1, 2] <- A[2, 1] <- A[2, 3] <- A[3, 2] <- 1 A[3, 4] <- A[4, 3] <- 1 nug <- nug_from_adj_mat(A, seed = 42L)  # Standalone model model <- mdgm_model(nug, dag_type = "spanning_tree") result <- mcmc(model, z_init = c(0L, 0L, 1L, 1L),                psi_init = 0.5, n_iter = 100L) result$summary() } # }

what: mdgm: Mixture of Directed Graphical Models — mdgm
title: mdgm: Mixture of Directed Graphical Models — mdgm
text: Bayesian inference discrete spatial random fields using mixtures directed graphical models (MDGM). package provides MCMC sampling directed acyclic graph (DAG) structures—including spanning trees acyclic orientations—optional emission models hierarchical observation processes.

title: mdgm: Mixture of Directed Graphical Models — mdgm
text: Standalone: spatial field \(z\) observed directly. MCMC updates DAG structure dependence parameter \(\psi\). Hierarchical: latent spatial field \(z\) generates observations \(y\) emission distribution (e.g., Bernoulli). MCMC additionally updates \(z\) emission parameters.

title: mdgm: Mixture of Directed Graphical Models — mdgm
text: Graph construction: nug_from_edge_list(), nug_from_adj_list(), nug_from_adj_mat() Model specification: mdgm_model() MCMC inference: mcmc()

title: mdgm: Mixture of Directed Graphical Models — mdgm
text: Carter, J. B. Calder, C. . (2024). Mixture Directed Graphical Models Discrete Spatial Random Fields. doi:10.48550/arXiv.2406.15700

title: mdgm: Mixture of Directed Graphical Models — mdgm
text: Maintainer: Brandon Carter brandon.carter@duke.edu

title: Create an MDGM model — mdgm_model
text: Constructs mixture directed graphical models discrete spatial random field defined undirected graph.

title: Create an MDGM model — mdgm_model
code: mdgm_model(   nug,   dag_type = c("spanning_tree", "acyclic_orientation", "rooted"),   n_colors = 2L,   emission = NULL )

title: Create an MDGM model — mdgm_model
text: nug NaturalUndirectedGraph object defining spatial structure. dag_type DAG construction type: "spanning_tree", "acyclic_orientation", "rooted". n_colors Number categories spatial field (default 2). emission Optional emission family hierarchical models: "bernoulli", "gaussian", "poisson". NULL (default), creates standalone model spatial field observed directly.

title: Create an MDGM model — mdgm_model
text: MdgmModel object.

title: Create an MDGM model — mdgm_model
code: # Standalone model on a triangle graph edges <- rbind(c(1, 2), c(2, 1), c(2, 3), c(3, 2), c(1, 3), c(3, 1)) nug <- nug_from_edge_list(3, edges, seed = 42L) model <- mdgm_model(nug, dag_type = "spanning_tree") model$nvertices() #> [1] 3 model$has_emission() #> [1] FALSE

what: Create an undirected graph from an adjacency matrix — nug_from_adj_mat
title: Create an undirected graph from an adjacency matrix — nug_from_adj_mat
text: Create undirected graph adjacency matrix

title: Create an undirected graph from an adjacency matrix — nug_from_adj_mat
code: nug_from_adj_mat(adj_mat, ...)

title: Create an undirected graph from an adjacency matrix — nug_from_adj_mat
text: adj_mat square symmetric matrix. Nonzero entries indicate edges; values used edge weights. Self-loops allowed. ... Additional arguments: seed (integer) reproducible RNG.

title: Create an undirected graph from an adjacency matrix — nug_from_adj_mat
text: NaturalUndirectedGraph object.

title: Create an undirected graph from an adjacency matrix — nug_from_adj_mat
code: # 4-vertex path graph from adjacency matrix A <- matrix(0, 4, 4) A[1, 2] <- A[2, 1] <- 1 A[2, 3] <- A[3, 2] <- 1 A[3, 4] <- A[4, 3] <- 1 g <- nug_from_adj_mat(A) g$nedges() #> [1] 3

what: Create an undirected graph from an edge list — nug_from_edge_list
title: Create an undirected graph from an edge list — nug_from_edge_list
text: Create undirected graph edge list

title: Create an undirected graph from an edge list — nug_from_edge_list
code: nug_from_edge_list(n, edges, ...)

title: Create an undirected graph from an edge list — nug_from_edge_list
text: n Number vertices. edges Edge list matrix data frame two three columns. first two columns vertex indices (1-indexed). undirected edge must appear twice (per direction). optional third column gives edge weights. ... Additional arguments: seed (integer) reproducible RNG.

title: Create an undirected graph from an edge list — nug_from_edge_list
text: NaturalUndirectedGraph object.

title: Create an undirected graph from an edge list — nug_from_edge_list
code: # Triangle graph: vertices 1-2-3 edges <- rbind(c(1, 2), c(2, 1), c(2, 3), c(3, 2), c(1, 3), c(3, 1)) g <- nug_from_edge_list(3, edges) g$nvertices() #> [1] 3 g$neighbors(1) #> [1] 2 3

what: Create a regular 2D grid graph — nug_from_grid
title: Create a regular 2D grid graph — nug_from_grid
text: Generates grid graph specified dimensions neighborhood order. Order 1 gives rook adjacency (4-connected), order 2 gives queen adjacency (8-connected).

title: Create a regular 2D grid graph — nug_from_grid
code: nug_from_grid(nrows, ncols, order = 1L, ...)

title: Create a regular 2D grid graph — nug_from_grid
text: nrows Number rows grid. ncols Number columns grid. order Neighborhood order: 1 rook (default) 2 queen. ... Additional arguments: seed (integer) reproducible RNG.

title: Create a regular 2D grid graph — nug_from_grid
text: NaturalUndirectedGraph object.

title: Create a regular 2D grid graph — nug_from_grid
code: # 4x4 rook grid g <- nug_from_grid(4, 4) g$nvertices() #> [1] 16 g$nedges() #> [1] 24
